intro: The clearest split is that 1Password has a free tier while Cycode does not. 1Password is Secrets Management tool for developers; Cycode is Software supply chain security platform scanning secrets, IaC, SCA, and SAST across the entire. The data points 1Password at teams with many api keys/tokens and ci/cd pipelines needing secure env vars, and Cycode at teams with many api keys/tokens and ci/cd pipelines needing secure env vars. Pricing: 1Password is subscription from $2.99; Cycode is subscription from $1. Lock-in is medium for 1Password and medium for Cycode, with DX at 4/5 vs 3/5 and transparency at 5/5 vs 2/5. On listed strengths, 1Password emphasises teams with many api keys/tokens and ci/cd pipelines needing secure env vars, and Cycode emphasises teams with many api keys/tokens and ci/cd pipelines needing secure env vars. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: neither fits solo, static projects, so if that is your context, look elsewhere; otherwise pick on the pivotal factor above and the DX score that matters most to your team.
quick_take: 1Password is for teams; Cycode is for teams; decide based on whether a free tier matters.
verdict_a: Choose 1Password if you want to start on a free tier before paying and your workload matches teams. Skip it if your context is solo, static projects.
verdict_b: Choose Cycode if starting on a paid subscription upfront is acceptable and your workload matches teams. Skip it if your context is solo, static projects.

faq.a: Listed entry pricing is $2.99 for 1Password and $1 for Cycode. 1Password offers a free tier while Cycode does not, so initial cost favors 1Password. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from 1Password to Cycode?
faq.a: 1Password is tagged with medium lock-in (items exportable to 1pux/json but deeply integrated workflows). Cycode is tagged with medium lock-in (supply chain saas, replaceable). Plan for SDK replacement, data export, and a parallel-run period; the dataset does not include migration tooling details.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, 1Password or Cycode?
faq.a: The dataset rates 1Password at 4/5 for developer experience and Cycode at 3/5, so 1Password scores higher. That score reflects overall polish rather than any single integration, so trial both against your stack before committing.

faq.q: Is Cycode a good alternative to 1Password?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches teams, since both list the same fit. Cycode is flagged as not for solo, static projects, so confirm your context does not fall there. Note that moving from 1Password to Cycode means leaving a free tier behind.
